overview: Boros Initiative is a fast, aggressive Legacy deck that establishes early board control using Chalice of the Void and Fable of the Mirror-breaker. It generates token pressure through Forth Eorlingas! and advances the Dungeon to create massive creatures or finish games. The deck relies on a tight 18-land mana base for consistency and adjusts its sideboard heavily against Reanimator and control strategies.
win_conditions: Summoning multiple Eorlingas tokens to deal combat damage
Advancing Dungeon stages to create large creatures or trigger lethal effects
Using Fable of the Mirror-breaker to clear the board and generate value
Establishing early Initiative or Monarch to restrict opponent actions

keep_criteria: Hands with early Chalice of the Void and Fable of the Mirror-breaker
Hands capable of generating immediate pressure and holding back opponents
Hands containing Solitude to answer early threats
Keep hands with 2+ lands or 1 land + 2 fast mana sources, plus a turn-1 or turn-2 play.
Prioritize hands that can cast Trinisphere by turn 2 or 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only sideboard hate cards like Rest in Peace but no pressure
Hands lacking early interaction or mana sources
Mulligan hands with no fast mana, no lands, or multiple low-impact cards.

examples.reason: Provides early board control, mana acceleration, and a strong turn 1 play despite losing the monkey to Thoughtseize.
The 7-card hand had only 1 Rest in Peace without pressure, so it couldn't be kept.
The 5-card hand was 7 lands, offering no spells to play.

early_game: Play Chalice of the Void on turn 1 to lock mana or creatures
Cast Fable of the Mirror-breaker or Forth Eorlingas! on turn 1-2
Use Solitude to remove early threats like Troll of Khazad-dûm
Establish Initiative or Monarch to restrict opponent's turns
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lotus Petal or Chrome Mox.
Turn 2: Use fast mana to cast Trinisphere if possible, or develop board with early threats.
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit Trinisphere's mana tax while maintaining your own acceleration.
mid_game: Advance Dungeon stages with Seasoned Dungeoneer to create blockers or attackers
Use Fable chapters to clear opponent's board and generate Treasures
Maintain board presence with Adventurers and Dungeoneers
Sideboard out vulnerable creatures against Reanimator decks
Conserve fast mana for key turns (Trinisphere, big threats).
Use Chrome Mox for color fixing or extra mana for spells.
Manage hand size to avoid flooding.
Commit threats only when you have protection or can follow up.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or hard counters.
late_game: Leverage Dungeon's Forge and Explore stages for massive stat boosts
Swarm the board with Eorlingas tokens to overwhelm defenses
Use accumulated Treasures to cast additional spells or generate copies
Manage mana efficiently with Sunbaked Canyon to avoid flooding
or combo pieces like Goblin Bombardment + Krenko), or Trinisphere lock combined with board presence.
Win by overwhelming the opponent's mana denial or out-resourcing them.
Go all-in when you have Trinisphere on the board, the opponent is locked out of key spells, and you have a clear path to lethal or a win condition.
Also commit when you can protect your threats or have removal for their answers.

key_cards.name: Chalice of the Void
key_cards.notes: Played on turn 1 to lock 0 or 1 mana/creatures, severely hampering opponent's development.

key_cards.name: Fable of the Mirror-breaker
key_cards.notes: Generates tokens, clears opponent's board, and provides continuous value through its chapters.

key_cards.name: Forth Eorlingas!
key_cards.notes: Creates 2-3 knights to contest Monarch/Initiative, block threats, and deal combat damage.

key_cards.name: Seasoned Dungeoneer
key_cards.notes: Advances Dungeon stages to create large creatures or finish games, but must be protected from Reanimate.

key_cards.name: Solitude
key_cards.notes: Answers early threats like Troll of Khazad-dûm or Living Weapon, buying time for the deck's strategy.

sequencing_tips: Play Chalice of the Void before exposing Cavern of Souls to avoid enabling opponent's Reanimate
Use Simian Spirit Guide to pay for potential Daze on turn 2
Open with City of Traitors for Chalice to avoid exposing Cavern of Souls early
Advance Dungeon carefully to avoid giving opponent reanimation targets
Use Fable chapters to generate Goblin Shaman copies for extra blockers and Treasures
be mindful of your own mana development.
Fast mana can lead to flooding, so balance land and spell ratios.
Watch for opponent's sideboard cards that target your strategy.
Common mistake: overcommitting to Trinisphere without follow-up threats.

matchups.advice: Sideboard out Caves of Chaos Adventurer and Seasoned Dungeoneer to avoid providing good targets for Reanimate, switching to a more Control mode.
Keep some Initiative creatures in the sideboard despite reanimation risk, play Chalice on 1 and Eorlingas on 2, and use Simian Spirit Guide for Daze protection.
Sideboard out Chalice of the Void as they can be invalidated by Flickerwisp, use Archon of Emeria to delay development, and exchange creatures with Fury and Loran.
Remove Petal if opponent lacks Wasteland, play Chalice on 1 and Explorer on 2 to prevent graveyard setup.
Keep hate pieces to hold back the combo, play Chalice for 0 and Fable on turn 1, and establish Initiative on turn 2.
Call in cavalry with Forth Eorlingas! on turn 1 to defend the Monarch and contest board control.

Use cantrips, multiple threats, or cost reducers to overcome counters. Play around key counters by holding up mana or using uncounterable threats. Consider artifact destruction if counters are tied to artifacts.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use removal on key ramp pieces. Trinisphere can lock them out if they overcommit to mana rocks.
Stabilize with removal, board wipes, or early threats. Use Trinisphere to slow their mana development. Prioritize surviving turn 1-3 to set up your game plan.
Pressure with early threats and cantrips to find answers. Use Trinisphere to lock out their interaction. Out-value them with card advantage and efficient threats.
Win the resource war. Prioritize keeping Trinisphere online. Use disruption to break their mana base. Out-value them with better sequencing and threat density.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), hard counters (Force of Will, Daze), and combo win conditions.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: this archetype has risen to the top tiers of the Legacy metagame, filling the power vacuum left by the banning of White Plume Adventurer

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: King Théoden summoned 4 Eorlingas to finish the game
the 3rd stage of it, Trap! , would have killed me
Fable of the Mirror-breaker on the first turn, and Initiative on the second turn

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: I made a wrong decision to open with City of Traitors for Chalice of the Void on 1 (which got countered) to avoid exposing Cavern of Souls
Simian Spirit Guide in hand to pay for a potential Daze on turn 2

avoiding attacks from the black Incarnation by the possibility of creating copies of the Goblin Shaman token to generate an extra blocker and using the copies generated at the end of the turn to chip away at life points and accumulate Treasures

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: wrecking my hand with Grief and Reanimate
interaction between Troll of Khazad-dûm and Reanimate
He opened with a big headache for me: Mother of Runes
combination of Thalia, Guardian of Thraben , Flickerwisp , and Umezawa’s Jitte
I was punished with a Null Rod
Archon of Cruelty , discarded due to excessive cards in hand
Archon of Emeria fell to Sheoldred’s Edict , Abrupt Decay took down Magus of the Moon
